patterns.context: trivial diffs (≤3 files, ≤50 lines, no security, domain≠high) generate false-positive noise when run through full adversarial review
patterns.pattern: scope gate in step-01 classifies diffs as minimal (Blind Hunter only) or full (all reviewers + personas). prevents wasted review cycles on trivial changes.

patterns.context: zero-finding reviews risk rubber-stamp approvals — reviewers may have been too shallow
patterns.pattern: forced-finding protocol: zero findings triggers ONE re-analysis with 'look harder' prompt. if still zero, accept as clean with double-checked note. prevents lazy approvals without infinite loops.

patterns.applicable_when: designing adversarial review systems with multiple reviewer perspectives
patterns.context: pure overlay (persona on existing reviewer) dilutes attention. pure separate subagents duplicate infrastructure and violate max-3-agent rule.
patterns.pattern: hybrid two-phase model: Phase A runs base reviewers unchanged, Phase B runs separate persona subagents informed by Phase A findings. personas go deeper (not wider), don't repeat base findings, and can upgrade WARN to FAIL with evidence.
